title: icy cold japanese soba
description: A light and refreshing Japanese dish from the Kanto region, featuring earthy soba noodles, a crispy egg wash, and a robata-grill finish. This dish is perfect for warm days and is also raw-friendly.
instructions: Combine soba noodles and ice water in a large bowl. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Drain the noodles and cut them into shorter lengths.
In a small saucepan, combine salt, brown rice vinegar, and sesame oil. Heat over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warm.
In a separate pan, toast the sesame seeds over low heat, stirring frequently, until lightly browned.
Grill the edamame over medium heat until lightly charred.
In a small bowl, mix together egg wash and flour until smooth.
Brush the egg wash mixture onto the soba noodles.
Place the noodles on the grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until lightly browned.
Transfer the noodles to a serving platter and top with green onions, toasted sesame seeds, and grilled edamame.
